title: Resolve July Workshop Registrations
deliverable: Direct registrant email copy, branded HTML code block, and response tracker.
context: The July workshop is being reworked because registration numbers are small. Current registrants need a clear and respectful direct message with options.
nextSteps: Review the current Zeffy registrant list.
Confirm what Richard wants to learn from each registrant.
Draft a warm message from Richard.
Offer the correct option: 1:1 session, future workshop transfer, or refund.
Have Monae review the final communication before sending.
prompt: Use DSG Email Brief Builder to create a Zeffy HTML email from Richard to current July workshop registrants. Explain that the workshop is being reworked because registration numbers are small. Offer three options: 1:1 session, transfer to a future workshop, or refund. Richard also wants to learn who each registrant is and what they are interested in beyond the original workshop signup. Return plain text email copy, a branded HTML code block, subject line options, preview text, and a short review checklist. Keep the tone warm, clear, concise, and not overwhelming.

title: Send Team Next-Steps Brief
deliverable: Team-facing next-steps email with owner, support, approver, due date, deliverable, links, and save location for each task.
context: The team needs clear communication after the June 29 board meeting. The email should help people know how to move tasks forward without overwhelming them.
nextSteps: Use the current Team Next Steps page as the task source.
Keep each task in the simple writing-block structure.
Include only the links needed for each task.
Add a short section summarizing what Andrew is carrying.
prompt: Create a DSG team next-steps brief using the simple writing-block format. Include every current task, and for each task include context, deliverable, owner, support, approves, due, next steps, and use-these-links. Keep it clear, calm, and actionable. Add a short bottom section summarizing what Andrew is carrying. Do not include a brand voice box.

title: Finalize June 29 Board Minutes
deliverable: Approved June 29 board minutes saved in the minutes folder.
context: The June 29 minutes are still draft until formally approved. They include board votes, compliance items, and assigned next steps.
nextSteps: Collect comments on the draft minutes.
prompt: Create a concise board-minute review reminder for DSG. The message should ask reviewers to check the June 29 draft minutes, resolve comments, and prepare for approval at the next team call. Include owner, support, approver, due date, and save location.

title: Move Compliance Filing Forward
deliverable: Filing/payment status update, saved confirmation if completed, and clarified ST-119.2 authority.
context: NY Charities registration/payment and sales-tax exemption clarification need follow-through. Some authority questions may require board confirmation.
nextSteps: Retry NY Charities filing/payment when the portal allows.
Post a short blocker/status note if the portal is unavailable.
prompt: Create a DSG compliance follow-up task brief. Include NY Charities filing/payment, ST-119.2 authority clarification, who owns it, who supports, who approves if unclear, the due date, where confirmations should be saved, and a short blocker update template.

title: Draft Role Clarity Materials
deliverable: Role descriptions, decision trees, and master org chart draft.
context: The team needs clearer operating roles before heavier tools or future program expansion.
nextSteps: Send to Andrew for first review.
prompt: Create a role clarity work plan for DSG. Include purpose, deliverables, owner, support, approver, due date, save location, and a simple checklist for role descriptions, decision trees, and org chart draft.

title: Schedule Workshop Post-Mortem and Marketing Funnel Review
deliverable: Scheduled review meeting and notes structure for workshop performance and next-cycle decisions.
context: Before new workshop dates are set, DSG needs a clear review of the July cycle, registration funnel, outreach, and whether outside marketing help is needed.
nextSteps: Identify what must change before future dates.
prompt: Create a DSG workshop post-mortem agenda and task brief. Include registration results, outreach review, funnel review, what worked, what failed, what needs to change, owner/support/approver, due date, save location, and a short decision log section.

title: Clean Up Finance and Admin Items
approves: Clark for finance records; Andrew for admin accounts
deliverable: Resolved or documented status for Relay receipt, Zeffy ACH, Workspace, Goodstack, and Zoom nonprofit setup.
context: Several finance/admin items need cleanup so records are accurate and platform setup is not left ambiguous.
nextSteps: Upload or forward the Facebook receipt to Relay.
Verify Goodstack and Zoom nonprofit status.
prompt: Create a DSG finance/admin cleanup checklist. Include owner, support, approvers, due date, save location, items to resolve, what to document, and a safe note that credentials should not be placed in shared docs.

title: Stage Funding Readiness Materials
support: Omari / Vaughn if guidance call moves forward
deliverable: Staged IRS letter, budget, program description, impact metrics, equity narrative, and concept note.
context: Funding opportunities are active, but materials should be staged carefully and sensitive language should be reviewed before external use.
nextSteps: Review Citizens NYC and other near-term fits.
Flag any identity or sponsor-benefit language for review.
Save materials in the fundraising folder.
prompt: Create a DSG funding readiness brief. Include materials to stage, owner/support/approver, due date, save location, sensitive language review note, and a short checklist for IRS letter, budget, program description, impact metrics, equity narrative, and concept note.
